text: Clean code is easy for other developers to read and understand. One key practice is using clear naming conventions: variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `doStuff`. It is also important to break code into small, focused functions. Each function should do one specific task, making it simpler to test and fix. The goal is to write code that is maintainable over time. When your code is clean, adding new features or fixing problems becomes much faster and less confusing for your team.
